source.title: Orbitofrontal cortex as a cognitive map of task space

confidence_rationale: Highly cited unifying theory paper (Niv/Schoenbaum collaboration) that proposes OFC supplies a state-space labeling for RL elsewhere in the brain. This is the canonical theoretical statement of the OFC-as-cognitive-map view that SD-033b's framing (ii) directly imports. Source quality very high; mapping fidelity strong because the paper's task-space abstraction is essentially what SD-033b calls the task-structure cognitive map. Confidence reduced slightly because the paper is theoretical synthesis rather than direct empirical demonstration -- empirical confirmation comes from Schuck 2016 and the Zhou/Stalnaker entries on MECH-263.
failure_signatures: If OFC's contribution to RL turned out to be value caching rather than state-space labeling, the cognitive-map framing would collapse and SD-033b's framing (ii) would not be supported.
If task states could be reliably inferred without OFC even when they are unobservable from sensory input, the proposed unique role of OFC in disambiguating hidden states would be falsified.

mapping.source_claim_statement: OFC encodes an abstraction of the agent's current task state -- including state information that is not directly observable from sensory input (such as task phase or working-memory content) -- and this state representation is consumed by reinforcement learning circuitry elsewhere in the brain. The framework explains classic OFC-lesion findings in reversal, devaluation, extinction, and delayed alternation, plus modulation of VTA dopamine RPE signals.
mapping.ree_translation: SD-033b framing (ii) -- the task-structure cognitive map -- is exactly the Wilson/Niv state-labeling function. In ree-v3 terms, this is the substrate that resolves which state z_self is in for purposes of E2's specific-outcome rollouts: when sensory observation underdetermines the state (partial observability), the OFC-analog disambiguates using stored task structure. SD-033b's claim that OFC-analog is E2's natural substrate address aligns with Wilson/Niv's claim that OFC supplies state labels to downstream RL.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Wilson/Niv frame the consumer of OFC state labels as VTA/striatal RL machinery (TD-learning over scalar value), while REE's E2 is a deterministic transition model over latent z. The functional role -- supplying state identity for downstream prediction -- maps cleanly, but the computational implementation in REE is not a TD value function over OFC states. SD-033c is the closer analog of the value-RL consumer; SD-033b is specifically the structured-prediction consumer. Treat the mapping as supporting the existence and function of the substrate, not as endorsing TD-RL as the implementation.
mapping.source_context: Theoretical synthesis paper covering rodent and primate OFC-lesion behaviour, OFC single-unit recordings, and rodent VTA dopamine modulation by OFC inactivation. Generates testable predictions to distinguish state-labeling from value-encoding accounts.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality very high -- Neuron paper from canonical Niv/Schoenbaum collaboration, foundational theoretical statement that has been empirically confirmed by Schuck 2016 (human fMRI) and the Zhou/Stalnaker rodent work in this same review directory. Mapping fidelity strong: the paper's state-labeling role is essentially SD-033b's framing (ii) verbatim. Transfer risk modest -- the paper is cross-species and the cognitive-map framing is cleanly architectural rather than species-specific.
